analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: fp32 (inferred: torch.randn default, no autocast; weights/inputs FP32)

bottleneck.description: The model is in eval() mode, so every nn.BatchNorm2d is a fixed affine transform (gamma/sqrt(var+eps) scale, beta-mean*scale shift). Because each convolution is dispatched to a cuDNN library kernel (conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc / 'Kernel2'), Inductor cannot fuse the BatchNorm into the conv epilogue and instead lowers each BN (and its ReLU6 clamp) into a standalone elementwise Triton kernel (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm_*). These kernels do a full-tensor DRAM read + affine + DRAM write with zero arithmetic reuse — pure memory traffic. There are at least 12 such kernels in the measure region (unattributed) plus more inside the prologue range, totaling ~132 us of avoidable elementwise/launch traffic.

transformation.description: Conv-BatchNorm folding at the Aten IR. In inference, fold each batch_norm that consumes a convolution output directly into the convolution's weight and bias: W' = W * (gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps)).reshape(-1,1,1,1); b' = beta + (b - running_mean) * gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps). After folding, the BN node is removed from the graph and its standalone Triton kernel disappears; the conv output then feeds the ReLU6 (hardtanh) clamp directly, which Inductor can pointwise-fuse into the (now sole) following elementwise op. This both eliminates ~12 elementwise kernels per forward and removes their full-tensor DRAM read/write traffic.
transformation.location: Custom FX pass over the Aten/Inductor pre-grad graph, registered ahead of Inductor lowering.
fx_steps: if bn.target not in (torch.ops.aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default, torch.ops.aten.batch_norm.default): continue
# unpack conv (input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups)
weight_bn, bias_bn, running_mean, running_var = bn.args[1], bn.args[2], bn.args[3], bn.args[4]; eps = bn.args[-1]
folded_b = bias_bn + (((conv_b if conv_b is not None else 0) - running_mean) * weight_bn * inv_std)
update conv weight/bias placeholders (register_buffer / get_attr) with folded_w, folded_b
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent to torch.ao.nn.intrinsic conv-bn fusion / torch.fx.experimental.optimization.fuse(model, inplace=True) applied in eval mode before Inductor; do the fold on the Aten graph so it covers all 6 conv->BN pairs.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes ~12 full-tensor read+write passes over the conv outputs (BN affine traffic). Approx 100+ GB-units of redundant DRAM read+write eliminated across the 3 blocks (BN kernels read every conv output element and write it back).

optimizations.notes: Foundational pass: it rewrites convolution weight/bias constants, so it MUST run before OPT-2 (1x1->matmul, which reshapes the folded weight) and OPT-3 (depthwise Triton fusion). Standard high-confidence inference transform. Folded weights are allocated at the runtime dtype (FP32 here); if a dtype-promotion pass were added it must precede this fold.

bottleneck.description: The 1x1 pointwise convolutions are dispatched to a cuDNN kernel ('Kernel2') that is severely occupancy/latency-bound, NOT compute-bound. A 1x1 conv is mathematically a GEMM (B*H*W, C_in) x (C_in, C_out) = (50176, C_in) x (C_in, C_out), which is the ideal regime for a cuBLAS tensor-core GEMM. cuDNN instead selected a kernel using 224-228 registers/thread and ~82 KB dynamic shared memory, which caps achieved_occupancy at ~8.3% and leaves eligible_cycles at 16-20% (latency-bound on Blackwell, the <20% indicator since warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable). Tensor cores fire only 39-45% of the time and DRAM throughput sits at 26-31%. The largest two (128->256, op_id 26 & 53) cost ~48 us each and are the biggest single kernels in the profile.

evidence.kernel: Kernel2 (cuDNN 1x1 pointwise; dominant k_00069 / k_00082 for 128->256)

transformation.description: Rewrite each 1x1, stride-1, padding-0, groups-1, dilation-1 convolution as an explicit GEMM so it routes to cuBLAS / Inductor's GEMM template instead of the 8%-occupancy cuDNN 'Kernel2'. Reshape activation from (N,C_in,H,W) to (N*H*W, C_in), reshape weight from (C_out,C_in,1,1) to (C_in,C_out)^T, do aten.mm/aten.addmm, then reshape back to (N,C_out,H,W). cuBLAS picks a tile/occupancy-balanced tensor-core GEMM, lifting occupancy and TC utilization well above the cuDNN choice for these large M=50176 problems.
transformation.location: Custom FX pass over the Aten pre-grad graph (after OPT-1 folding).
fx_steps: inp, w = node.args[0], node.args[1]; stride, pad, dil = node.args[3], node.args[4], node.args[5]; groups = node.args[8]
if not (kernel_hw(w)==(1,1) and stride==[1,1] and pad==[0,0] and dil==[1,1] and groups==1): continue
mm     = graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten.addmm.default, (bias, x_2d, w_t)) if bias is not None else graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten.mm.default, (x_2d, w_2d_T))
transformation.code_hint: Inductor has a conv-1x1-as-mm rewrite but only applies it under certain layout/heuristic conditions; forcing it here guarantees the cuBLAS GEMM path. Keep the activation in channels_last so the permute is a metadata-only view and the mm reads contiguous (M, C_in) rows (pairs well with OPT-4).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Same logical FLOPs, but a well-occupied cuBLAS GEMM raises achieved occupancy from ~8% toward 50-70% and tensor-core utilization from ~45% toward >80%, roughly halving the wall time of the two 48 us 128->256 kernels and the two 27 us 64->128 kernels.

optimizations.notes: Must run after OPT-1 (folds BN scale/shift into the conv weight that this pass reshapes). Capped at medium per fallback policy (exa-search/context7 unavailable to confirm the exact Blackwell cuBLAS tile choice). Estimate uses relative duration only; ncu counter inflation (2-5x) means absolute ns are not literal.

bottleneck.description: Each depthwise stage emits an Inductor layout-conversion kernel 'triton_poi_fused_convolution_0' (NCHW->channels-last copy feeding cuDNN's NHWC depthwise) that is pure bandwidth movement: 25 regs/thread, IPC 0.09, sm_throughput 9.6%, eligible_cycles ~9% (deeply latency-bound), tensor_core 0%. It exists only to satisfy the cuDNN depthwise layout. The depthwise conv itself (conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc) is correctly memory-bound (occ 72-87%, DRAM 48-74%, tc 0% as expected for depthwise) and is healthy — but it is preceded by this redundant copy and followed by a separate BN/ReLU6 kernel.

transformation.description: Force the depthwise 3x3 conv through Inductor's own Triton convolution codegen instead of cuDNN. Because the depthwise conv is memory-bound (tc 0%) and Inductor can pointwise-fuse the (already BN-folded) bias add and the ReLU6 hardtanh into the same Triton kernel, a single fused depthwise-conv+clamp Triton kernel replaces the {layout-copy + cuDNN depthwise + separate activation} trio, eliminating the redundant copy kernel and one elementwise launch per stage.
transformation.location: Inductor config (force convolution lowering for grouped/depthwise convs) plus the OPT-1 epilogue fusion; alternatively an explicit FX rewrite of aten.convolution(groups==C_in) to an Inductor-lowerable form.
fx_steps: if node.args[8] == channels_of(node.args[0]):   # groups == in_channels -> depthwise
mark node with meta['inductor_force_triton'] = True   # opt out of cuDNN dispatch
# ensure the following hardtanh(0,6) and any folded-bias add remain immediate users so Inductor's
# pointwise scheduler fuses them into the conv epilogue (no separate elementwise kernel)
set torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_conv_backends to prefer 'triton' for grouped convs
transformation.code_hint: The 'conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc' depthwise kernel is already efficient; the win is removing 'triton_poi_fused_convolution_0' (the NCHW->NHWC copy) and folding the activation. Validate that the Triton depthwise codegen does not regress the memory-bound stage before committing.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates the redundant full-tensor layout copy before each depthwise conv (1 copy read+write per stage) and folds the depthwise activation into the conv epilogue.

optimizations.notes: Must run after OPT-1 so the activation/bias fuse into the depthwise epilogue. Low confidence: Inductor's Triton depthwise codegen can be slower than cuDNN for some shapes; gate behind an autotune comparison. Largely overlaps with OPT-4 (channels_last also removes the copy).

bottleneck.description: The depthwise convs run NHWC (conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc) and the pointwise GEMM (after OPT-2) prefers row-contiguous channels-last activations, but the graph default is NCHW, forcing per-stage layout-conversion copies (triton_poi_fused_convolution_0, eligible_cycles 9%, sm 9.6%). Running the entire model in channels_last keeps a single consistent layout end-to-end so these copies collapse to metadata-only views.

transformation.description: Enforce channels_last (NHWC) for the whole subgraph: convert the graph input to channels_last once at the entry and let layout propagate so every depthwise (NHWC-native) and pointwise (GEMM) consumer reads its preferred layout without per-stage copies. At FX, insert one aten.clone with memory_format=channels_last at the placeholder and drop redundant intervening contiguous/copy nodes between consecutive convs.
transformation.location: FX pass over the Aten graph input + redundant-copy elimination between conv stages.
fx_steps: inp = next(n for n in graph.nodes if n.op == 'placeholder')
# remove now-redundant layout copies that Inductor inserted before each depthwise conv
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent at the module level to model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) and x = x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last); doing it in the graph guarantees Inductor keeps the layout stable across all 3 blocks.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Collapses the per-stage NCHW<->NHWC layout copies into metadata views; one read+write full-tensor copy removed per depthwise stage.

optimizations.notes: Independent of OPT-1/2 but most effective applied after OPT-2 so the GEMM permute becomes a no-op view. Overlaps with OPT-3's copy elimination; apply whichever the autotuner shows is cheaper, not both redundantly.

global_notes: 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 1.37% of attributed time): aten::cudnn_convolution op_id=6 (DW 3x3 32ch, 5856 ns, 0.68%) and op_id=33 (DW 3x3 32ch, 5920 ns, 0.69%).

ASSUMPTION: knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in this checkout (searched several candidate paths). Architecture context uses established Blackwell facts for the RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition: sm_count assumed = 188; warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable on Blackwell (confirmed null in every aggregated block) so eligible_cycles_pct < 20% is used as the latency-bound indicator. FX patterns use canonical Aten-IR surgery (conv-bn fold, conv1x1->mm, channels_last) per fallback policy; novel-pass confidence capped at medium.

EDGE CASE fused_kernel_double_count: every fine-grained per-op-id entry has is_fused == true, and the coarse aggregations 'layer::unique::prologue' (437850 ns, 30 kernels) and 'aten::cudnn_convolution_0' (160511 ns, 9 kernels) are NVTX/torch_profiler roll-ups that cover the SAME kernels as the fine-grained is_fused entries. Summing all non-null aggregated blocks (860312 ns, per spec) therefore double-counts: 'prologue' is essentially the first (warm-up) compiled iteration spanning start_ns 4.256e9-4.659e9, while the per-op-id measure kernels run from 4.659e9 onward. All bottleneck mechanisms and impact estimates are derived from the fine-grained measure-iteration kernels, not from the inflated sum.

EDGE CASE dynamic_shapes (FALSE POSITIVE): kernels sharing the name 'Kernel2' have durations spanning 13696-48480 ns (>50% CV), which trips the dynamic-shapes heuristic. This is NOT dynamic shaping — these are statically-distinct conv shapes (32->64, 64->128, 128->256) that happen to dispatch to the same cuDNN kernel symbol. No shape-specialization opportunity.
duration_ns values are inflated 2-5x by ncu counter collection and are used only for relative ranking within this profile, never as absolute latency.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (conv-BN fold) rewrites convolution weight/bias constants and is a prerequisite for OPT-2 (1x1->mm reshapes the folded weight) and OPT-3 (depthwise epilogue fusion folds the activation). OPT-4 (channels_last) is independent but best applied after OPT-2; OPT-3 and OPT-4 both eliminate the layout-copy kernel and should not both be applied. No cycles. Recommended order: OPT-1 -> OPT-2 -> OPT-4 (with OPT-3 as an autotune-gated alternative to OPT-4).

19 pre-NVTX initialization kernels (k_00000-k_00018: conv_depthwise2d_forward, bn_fw_inf, vectorized_elementwise, Kernel2) are flagged as warm-up in profile.warnings and are correctly excluded from the budget.
